(PDB-1455) Fix pe/foss inconsistencies and bug with using ruby detection in spec/rules.erb

other cases where PE and FOSS differ by removing as much variation as well.

Lots of incosistencies and bugs were found in this exercise:

(CPR-515) Allow separate tasks for install vs upgrade

Currently, the postinstall tasks from the packaged project
are executed unconditionally, both on install as well as
upgrades. This can cause issues, as in the case with puppetserver
potentially overwriting config values if the user has changed them
from the package defaults.

This adds an addtional set of tasks that can be executed in the
pack

I'd really like to move away from FPM and use native packaging mechanisms where possible. If you use that then at least for RPMs you could use [packit](https://packit.dev) to create RPMs for every PR to  and run end-to-end tests with it using [Testing Farm](https://docs.testing-farm.io).

(SERVER-70) Update systemd template for nonzero exit codes

Because of the way that java handles the TERM signal (which is what
systemd uses by default), systemd considers the stoppage to have failed
and puts the service in a failed state. This is because our clojure
libraries don't catch or handle the TERM signal, but we can work around
it in the service file for the time being. This commit adds

(EZ-97) Terminate start / reload waiting loop if process dies

This commit changes the start and reload commands to exit immediately if
the service process dies while waiting for the command's action to
complete.  Previously, the commands would continue to loop unnecessarily
for up to the configured start_timeout (for start) or start_timeout / 10
(for reload) before exiting with an error.

(EZ-52) Add custom created directories to the rpm

Previously in ezbake, custom directories would be added to the buildroot
for rpm, but would not be added to the %files section of the rpm, which
means they would not be included in the package generated. This commit
adds %dir declarations for each dir in create_dirs, and assigns it the
same permissions and ownership that the install.sh postinst wi

(EZ-97) Replace ==, =, and != with -eq, -eq, and -ne in start and reload

This commit replaces usages of ==, =, and != with -eq, -eq, and -ne
where numeric comparisons are being made in if statements in the start
and reload scripts.

(PE-7614) Fix SLES-11 pe-puppetserver init script

Without this patch the pe-puppetserver service in PE fails to start on SLES-11.

The root cause of the issue is that the JAVA_ARGS string contains
sub-strings which are intended to be positional arguments.  Because the
complete string is used when invoking a command, BASH expands the string
into positional arguments without considering the inner q

(maint) Do not use virtual package for java dep

Prior to this commit, we were requiring virtual packages to satisfy java
runtime dependencies on deb based platforms. This was fine until we get
to Ubuntu 16.04 Xenial. On Xenial, Java7, Java8, and Java9 all provide
java8-runtime-headless. This is a problem, because it means that java9
is automatically installed to satisfy the specified runtime requ

(SERVER-3) Update EL init scripts to invoke the daemon from INSTALL_DIR

On el systems, if the service is invoked using init.d directly, while in
a root owned directory, it will fail to run several external commands
during startup. This isn't a problem for debian because we pass in a
directory to run from that will be accessible to the service user. This
commit implements the same behavior for EL

(SERVER-338) Add net-tools dependency for service unit files

Without this patch the init scripts and unit files make calls to
netstat, which may or may not exist on the system.  This is a problem
because the service will fail to start if netstat is not present.  This
patch addresses the problem by expressing net-tools as a dependency of
the packages which will ensure netstat is present prior to s

(EZ-103) Address incorrect rundir perms

Prior to this commit, we had been creating the rundir, but sometimes
with permissions which prevented the service from being able to create
its own pidfile.  In these cases, the Java process could be launched
successfully and the service start command could still return success.
Any subsequent attempts to ask the service framework for status on the
service,

(EZ-97) Kill background Java process if start script terminated

This commit will trap HUP, INT, and TERM signals sent to the start
script.  If any is trapped, the associated Java process that the start
script spawned will be terminated.  This is done so that if a 'restart'
is issued while a start is already in process, the Java process for the
first start attempt will be cleaned up before the sec

(EZ-97) Remove pidfile just before start

This commit removes an old pidfile for a service process just before
starting up a new process.  Previously, if an old pid file had been in
place but the start failed, the underlying service script might
interpret the result of the start as 'success' when it should be
'failed'.

Explicitly check out main branch during backports

The v7 release of  introduced a major change where it
will refuse to check out repository state that may be mutated by the
pull request. This commit explicitly sets  on the checkout
step of the workflow as  has logic for finding
the pull request and cherry-picking commits out of it.

Signed-off-by: Charlie Sharpsteen <charlie.com>

(SERVER-1670) Stop autogenerating dependencies for RPM packages

Currently the generated RPM packages will automatically generate
a set of requirements based on the contents of the package. This
has become an issue with puppet-server 2.7.0, as adding gems
to the package inadvertently added a dependency on system ruby.

This adds 'AutoReq: 0' to the RPM specs to stop generating automatic
dependency

(SERVER-2602) Do not manage egd

This setting is of little value in Java 8+ and we've seen no difference
anecdotally between explicitly configuring /dev/urandom as a source vs
using the JVMs defaults.

We will be explicitly setting this to /dev/random for FIPS enabled
builds, however we will do so via the JAVA_ARGS variable. FOSS users who
would like to manage their egd source should use JAVA_ARGS

Refactor code for copying files out of jars

Prior to this commit, there was a ton of overlap in how
we were copying config files out of the upstream jars
vs. how we were copying doc files out of the upstream jars.
Since there may be some more of these kinds of files coming
into play, I thought now would be a good time to refactor

(MAINT) Fix init script bug when no post_start_action specified.

By default the post_start_action is an empty list which is a logical 'true'
value in Ruby. The previous logic led to an empty  block in the init scripts
which is a syntax error in sh and probably other shell script interpreters.

(maint) Remove OnOutOfMemoryError from systemd service files

The kill in the OnOutOfMemoryError setting in the systemd service files
causes systemd to try to run the command for some reason. Possibly
something to do with escaping quotes. Until this can be fixed, this
commit removes those settings from the systemd service definitions.

This is not a major issue because long running migrations are so infrequently run, but just wanted to call it out.

(EZ-132) Change Java runtime OOM and logfile behavior

I think this should be all that's needed. I changed

  -XX:OnOutOfMemoryError="kill -9 %p"
to
  -XX:+CrashOnOutOfMemoryError

CrashOnOutOfMemoryError has been around since Java 8, so this should work with all our products.

I also added:

    -XX:ErrorFile="${app_logdir}/${real_name}_err_pid%p.log"

(maint) Interpolate EZBake::Config[:project] correctly

Previously the literal string "EZBake::Config[:project]" was printed for
all rake task invocations. I imagine this was intended to be the value
of the project name instead. This commit updates the line to correctly
interpolate the variable in the string.

This commit improves the places where ezbake calls into
pomegranate/aether to make sure that we are passing through
the value for the  setting from the project (or
from the user profile), so that aether will look for artifacts

(EZ-72) Support split bootstrap in configuration

This commit adds support to ezbake for splitting a project's bootstrap files
between /etc/puppetlabs/{project}/services.d/ and
/opt/puppetlabs/server/apps/{project}/config/services.d/

This serves the ultimate goal of putting user-configurable service entries
in one location, and the services that should not be modified in another.

Rename stage.clj to core.clj

As the main clj now contains both stage and build logic, it no longer
makes sense to name it stage.clj. This commit renames it to core.clj, as
seems to be common in clojure projects.

(EZ-35) Make use of project build-scripts

This commit adds a method to copy build-scripts from the current project to the
staging dir. Previously, we only copied build-scripts from direct dependencies,
which resulted in the FOSS puppetserver build-scripts to be used instead of the
pe-puppet-server-extensions build-scripts when building from pe-pse instead of
from pe-puppetserver (PE-28810).

(SERVER-1247) fix bug with services-d bootstrap on systemd

In systemd, it doesn't appear that variable interpolation is supported
inside of the defaults files.  Prior to this commit, when using
the  option for bootstrap configuration, we were generating
a defaults file that relied on interpolation.  This commit changes
the code to avoid the interpolation.

(maint) Undo some bad assumptions from packaging hard-coding

For debian repos with no apt_repo_name set, the packaging tasks that
generate the repo look for artifacts under deb/dist, rather than
deb/dist/main as it had originally appeared. This fixes the code to
match the assumptions in packaging.
